known_targets.notes: Confirmed and suspected wreck locations. Use these as scan anchors.

entries.name: Michigan City, IN — Cluster
entries.notes: Highest detection count in baseline scan. Optical+thermal agreement. Near Michigan City harbor — plausible wreck zone.

entries.name: Racine/Kenosha, WI
entries.notes: Moderate detection count. Worth re-scanning with tighter bbox.

entries.name: Andaste / Monster Candidate
entries.notes: 330ft steel freighter candidate. Deep water (450ft). Thermal spine-lock candidate. Not yet scanned with current pipeline.

entries.name: Gilcher (Fox Islands)
entries.notes: Known wreck near Fox Islands. Wooden freighter, 220ft. Good for thermal baseline testing.

entries.name: Parnell (Beaver Islands)
entries.notes: Well-documented wreck. Excellent for triple-lock calibration. Depth 180ft.

entries.name: Lumberman (50ft depth, confirmed dive site)
entries.notes: Known dive site at 50ft (15m). Surface boat detected via B08 NIR (z=11.34) Sep 16 2025 - B08 does NOT penetrate water. Need B02 Blue (458-523nm) for water penetration to 15-25m depth. Use Stumpf ratio: Depth = m1 * ln(n * R_blue) / ln(n * R_green) + m0

scan_lessons.notes: What we've learned from running scans. Parameter tweaks that worked or failed.

what_worked: Per-sensor band filtering eliminates cross-contamination between thermal/optical/SAR
2-lock (2 of 3 sensors) is practical threshold — 3-lock too strict for current data
AWS public S3 buckets (sentinel-s1-rtc-indigo, sentinel-s2-l2a, usgs-landsat) are reliable data sources
KoboldCPP via Cloudflare tunnel works as LLM backend — no cloud API dependency
Landsat 8/9 thermal B10/B11 from AWS works for anomaly detection
SAR RTC GeoTIFFs from AWS process correctly on Xenon GPU
what_failed: SWOT sensor — no data available for Great Lakes region
Wide bbox (entire Lakes MI/Huron) produces too many false positives
All 3 sensors running same script without band filtering = identical results (fixed)
Syncthing not configured — data transfer Pi↔Xenon manual
HyP3 job submission failed on payload format (fixed: needs 'jobs' array)
Earthdata auth failed with 'cesarops.com.@' username — correct is 'cesarops.com' with password '@Juliek01241973'

parameter_recommendations.note: SAR has very high anomaly counts (1M+) — may need higher z-score or different threshold. Optical B01 (aerosol) produces noise — consider excluding.

what_worked: Full pipeline: thermal (1050) + optical (1050) + SAR (1100) all processed without crash
Fusion found same 4 targets as baseline — reproducible results
KoboldCPP LLM interpretation via Cloudflare tunnel
what_failed: SAR GeoTIFFs from AWS bucket are Gulf of Mexico data, not Great Lakes — all 1100 SAR detections at lat~29 lon~-89
No real SAR data for Great Lakes available yet
Optical has coordinate outliers (one detection in Papua New Guinea) — some JP2/Fmask files have bad geotransforms
No 3-lock possible without real SAR data

parameter_recommendations.note: Focus on optical+thermal (2-lock) until real SAR data obtained. Silt plume detection via optical is best path forward.

what_worked: B02 Blue (458-523nm) downloaded for 3 tiles (TER, TFR, TFS)
what_failed: 0 anomalies at z>=5 across all 3 B02 tiles
Date is 2015-03-27 - late winter, not optimal for water penetration
Tiles are TER/TFR/TFS - NOT the Lumberman tile (TDN)
B02 on Lumberman tile (16TDN) not available in CMR for 2015-2016
Need Copernicus download for S2C_16TDN_20250916 B02/B03

parameter_recommendations.note: B02 needs to be combined with B03 for Stumpf ratio depth analysis

data_sources.notes: Working data sources with their quirks. Agent should reference this before downloading.

aws_sentinel1_rtc.status: BROKEN — contains Gulf of Mexico data, not Great Lakes
aws_sentinel1_rtc.note: Tile 16RBT is Gulf of Mexico (lat~29-30, lon~-89). Need to find correct Great Lakes tiles or use HyP3 processing.

aws_landsat.notes: RequesterPayer flag required. Landsat 8/9. Summer 2025 data available for Path 020.

aws_sentinel2.notes: Level 2A (atmospherically corrected). JP2 format, not GeoTIFF. Tile 16TDN has 2025 data.

needs_setup.copernicus_dataspace: Needs username/password. Sentinel-1 SLC + Sentinel-2 MSI.
needs_setup.hyp3_rtc: Earthdata JWT works. Submit SLC granules for cloud RTC processing. Takes 1-6 hours.
needs_setup.sar_real_data: Need real SAR GeoTIFFs for Great Lakes. Options: 1) HyP3 submit jobs (Earthdata JWT works, just need correct granule names), 2) Find correct ASF RTC bucket tile IDs for Great Lakes, 3) Copernicus Sentinel-1 download with SNAP processing.

techniques_to_try.notes: Research ideas and techniques worth testing. Agent should pick one per idle week.

entries.name: Post-storm silt plume analysis for wreck detection
entries.notes: After storms with 20-40mph winds, silt plumes settle over wrecks. Use optical (Sentinel-2 B04/B08) and existing HLS data. Works WITHOUT SAR — primary detection method until real SAR data arrives.

entries.name: VV/VH polarization ratio for SAR wreck detection
entries.notes: Ratio of VV to VH backscatter may highlight metallic structures better than single-polarization. Steel wrecks have distinct polarization signatures. Requires real Great Lakes SAR GeoTIFFs first.

entries.name: Multi-temporal SAR change detection
entries.notes: Compare SAR images from different dates. Persistent anomalies across dates = real targets. Single-date anomalies = likely noise.

entries.name: NDWI (Normalized Difference Water Index) from Sentinel-2
entries.notes: NDWI = (Green - NIR) / (Green + NIR). Helps distinguish submerged objects from water column. Use B03 (green) and B08 (NIR).

entries.name: Thermal time-series analysis
entries.notes: Steel wrecks retain heat differently than surrounding water. Compare thermal anomalies across multiple dates to find persistent cold/hot spots.

entries.name: Depth-based filtering
entries.notes: Filter detections by bathymetry. Known wrecks are typically in 100-500ft depth. Exclude shallow (<30ft) and ultra-deep (>600ft) anomalies.

entries.name: Blue/Green water penetration for shallow wrecks (<100ft)
entries.status: active - search planner can find dates
entries.notes: At 50ft depth, NIR (B08) cannot penetrate water. Must use B02 Blue (458-523nm) and B03 Green (543-578nm) which penetrate water column. Lumberman wreck structure should show as dark anomaly in blue/green against surrounding water. Target: 42.8476, -87.82946.

entries.name: B02/B03 water penetration for 50ft wrecks
entries.notes: For Lumberman at 50ft: B02 Blue (458-523nm) penetrates water column best. B03 Green (543-578nm) second. B04 Red moderate. B08 NIR = surface only, useless for submerged wrecks. Current data has NO B02/B03 for 16TDN Sep 2025 - must download from CMR or AWS.

entries.name: B02/B03 water penetration for shallow wrecks
entries.notes: For Lumberman (50ft): Need B02 Blue (458-523nm) + B03 Green (543-578nm). B02 penetrates water column best. Currently NO B02/B03 data for 16TDN Sep 2025 in cache - must download from CMR.

entries.name: B02/B03 water penetration for shallow wrecks
entries.notes: For 50ft wrecks: B02 Blue (458-523nm) and B03 Green (543-578nm) penetrate water. B08 NIR only sees surface. Current data has NO B02/B03 for tile 16TDN Sep 2025 - must download.

entries.name: B02 Blue (458-523nm) + B03 Green (543-578nm) for 50ft wrecks
entries.status: active - search planner can find dates
entries.notes: At 50ft depth, B08 NIR cannot penetrate water. Must use B02 Blue and B03 Green. Target: Lumberman (42.8476, -87.82946). B02 penetrates ~20m in clear water, B03 ~15m. Need HLS S30 tile 16TDN Sep 16 2025.

entries.name: SAR descending pass detection for Great Lakes wrecks
entries.status: active - search planner can find dates
entries.notes: Descending passes (~18:00 local) show better wreck detection in Great Lakes. Afternoon wind creates capillary waves, submerged steel creates smooth 'shadow' in wave field. Use VV/VH ratio >1.2, coherence >0.6, backscatter -15 to +5 dB.

entries.name: SAR descending pass (~18:00) for Great Lakes wrecks
entries.status: active - search planner can find dates
entries.notes: Descending passes show better wreck detection in Great Lakes. Afternoon wind creates capillary waves, submerged steel creates smooth shadow. VV/VH ratio >1.2, coherence >0.6, backscatter -15 to +5 dB.

entries.name: Download full S2C tile from Copernicus for Lumberman
entries.notes: Copernicus has S2C_MSIL2A_20250916T163941_N0511_R126_T16TDN (840MB). This has ALL bands including B02 Blue (458-523nm) for the exact Lumberman date. Download full product and extract B02/B03/B04/B08 for water penetration analysis. Product ID: 489d402d-d658-4d75-8d88-01144fbf8c4f

agent_behavior.notes: How the agent should behave. Baked-in instructions so it doesn't need to figure these out each time.
rules: Always check weather for the scan area before recommending optical/thermal scans
SAR works in all weather — recommend it when cloud cover > 50%
Start with known target coordinates before doing wide-area searches
If a scan finds nothing, try: (1) narrow bbox, (2) lower z-score, (3) add SAR sensor
If a scan finds too many detections, try: (1) raise z-score to 3.0, (2) require 3-lock instead of 2-lock
After each scan, update the knowledge_base.json with results
When idle (no active search), run background probes on known wreck sites to build baseline data
Pull one new research technique per week and test it on known targets

BEST SCANNING WINDOW: Check wind history — ideal conditions are AFTER a storm with sustained winds 20-40mph. This stirs up silt plumes that settle over wrecks, creating visible signatures in optical/SAR data. Scan 12-48 hours after the storm passes, when water is settling but silt is still suspended. Use web search to check: 'wind history Lake Michigan past 3 days' or 'NOAA buoy wind data Great Lakes'.

SILT PLUME DETECTION: Post-storm silt plumes are the #1 indicator for wreck location. Look for elongated sediment patterns in optical (Sentinel-2 B04/B08) and backscatter anomalies in SAR (VV polarization). Wrecks act as obstacles — silt deposits downstream create visible streaks.
Wind direction matters — scan DOWNWIND of suspected wreck sites after storms.
Use smart_search_planner.py --auto-plan to search ALL available data, score dates, and build optimal download+scan plan.

bathymetry.B02_Blue_458_523nm: PRIMARY depth penetration (~15-25m in clear water)
bathymetry.B04_Red_650_680nm: Surface reference (separate water column from bottom)
bathymetry.B08_NIR_785_900nm: Water surface/atmospheric correction - does NOT penetrate water

methods.stumpf_ratio: Depth = m1 * ln(n * R_blue) / ln(n * R_green) + m0
methods.lyzenga: Depth = a * ln(R_blue) + b * ln(R_green) + c

signatures.exposed_steel_hull: High VV, low VH (smooth metal = corner reflector)
signatures.submerged_wreck: Moderate VV, high VH (volume scattering through water)
signatures.oil_hydrocarbon_sheen: Very low VV, very low VH (smooth surface dampens capillary waves)
signatures.mussel_covered_wreck: Moderate VV, moderate VH (rough biological surface)

orbit_passes.ascending: ~06:00 local, 6-12 day revisit, morning surface, lower wind
orbit_passes.descending: ~18:00 local, 6-12 day revisit, BETTER for Great Lakes (afternoon wind creates capillary waves, submerged steel creates smooth shadow)
